---
title: "Agentic-Coding Evaluation Lab"
---

This site publishes statistical methods, evidence contracts, and report tooling for comparing
coding-agent configurations. It is a methodology and verification project, not an operational
benchmark or leaderboard.

## Current status

| Surface | Status |
|---|---|
| Core `0.2.0` | Repository-versioned and wheel-buildable; not tagged or published to a package registry |
| Successor method | Under review; no enforcing test, replicate policy, or practical-benefit rule selected |
| Contract B `2.0.0-draft.1` | Repository-only synthetic draft; independently recomputes `NOT_EVALUABLE`; no operational authority |
| Real evidence | No powered, scientifically admissible model comparison published |

The historical four-task live example is a transport-confounded, summary-only smoke diagnostic; its
raw attempt records are not published. The representative example is synthetic. Neither supports a
model-selection claim.

::: {.callout-important title="Current scientific result"}
Exact arithmetic makes a calculation reproducible; it does not establish inferential validity. The
experimental note shows that one exactly enumerated sign-flip construction exceeds its nominal
one-sided 5% level under part of the tested composite-null boundary. The alternative construction is
calibrated only for a narrower synthetic model. No successor method is selected.

[Read the executable experimental inference note](reports/experimental-inference-note.qmd).
:::

## Published specifications

- [Identity domains](identity-domain.qmd) — frozen v1 structural fields, canonicalization, and a
  cross-implementation conformance vector. Equality is necessary, not sufficient, for numerical
  comparability.
- [Experimental inference note](reports/experimental-inference-note.qmd) — executable synthetic
  decision input. It selects no method.
- [Contract B v2 refusal example](reports/contract-v2-not-evaluable.qmd) — synthetic
  registration-to-report verification that recomputes or refuses and cannot recommend promotion.

## Historical report examples

The rendered reports below exercise the reporting layer. Their evidence manifests identify them as
synthetic. They are not performance results.

- [Sample synthetic lab report](reports/sample/methodology-report.html)
- [Synthetic campaign report](reports/campaigns/synthetic-local-promotion-001/methodology-report.html)

## Evidence rules

- Every published number must trace to committed evidence bytes.
- Synthetic, representative, smoke, and measured evidence remain distinct.
- Copied facts retain their originating provenance and receipt.
- Missing or contradictory inputs produce refusal.
- Evidence inputs are recursively scanned in the parameterized report path; Contract B checks its
  bound public payload separately. No whole-repository scan is claimed.

## Remaining work

Before a powered claim is admissible, the project must complete the scientific design, owner-issued
runtime receipts, operational registration verification, grader validation, governed task selection,
A/A independence and noise-floor calibration, and operating-characteristic analysis.

See the repository [status](https://github.com/verlyn13/eval-lab-methodology/blob/main/STATUS.md),
[roadmap](https://github.com/verlyn13/eval-lab-methodology/blob/main/PLAN.md), and
[source](https://github.com/verlyn13/eval-lab-methodology) for details.
